symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid-onset tense abdominal distension (ascites) over ~2 weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New tense ascites is a defining feature of hepatic decompensation from portal hypertension.
Malignant ascites is an alternative cause requiring imaging/cytology to exclude.
Cardiac ascites occurs but is less prominent than peripheral oedema in isolated right heart failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral peripheral oedema to the shins
symptoms.character: puffy, pitting swelling of both ankles and lower legs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypoalbuminaemia plus fluid retention produces dependent oedema alongside ascites.
Bilateral pitting oedema is the shared feature that creates the cardiac anchoring trap.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthopnoea / breathlessness relieved by sitting upright
symptoms.character: cannot lie flat, must be propped upright to breathe; tachypnoeic per caregiver

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tense ascites splints the diaphragm and hepatic hydrothorax can form, producing orthopnoea.
Orthopnoea is classically cardiac — reinforcing the mandatory cardiac differential.

symptoms.symptom_name: Right upper quadrant dull ache with chronic fatigue and mild cognitive fog

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: RUQ discomfort (hepatic capsule) plus chronic fatigue and subtle cognitive change fit chronic liver disease, with fog raising possible minimal hepatic encephalopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cachexia / upper-body muscle wasting with abdominal distension (sarcopenia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sarcopenia is common in advanced cirrhosis; a partial version is video-observable but formal assessment needs examination.
Cachexia also accompanies malignancy — keeps that differential live.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stigmata of chronic liver disease — spider naevi, palmar erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cutaneous stigmata strongly point to chronic liver disease and help separate hepatic from cardiac causes — but they are a clinician exam finding, sealed and unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Remote intravenous drug use, never screened for hepatitis C

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Injecting drug use is the dominant transmission route for hepatitis C in Australia; the key aetiological clue for the underlying cirrhosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive fluid thrill and shifting dullness with tender hepatomegaly on abdominal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms gross ascites and hepatomegaly — but requires a clinician's hands; sealed and not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haematemesis or melaena at presentation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No active bleeding now — but portal hypertension keeps the risk high; a key safety-net screen, not a reassurance.
No flap currently, arguing against overt encephalopathy — but a clinician sign, and minimal encephalopathy can still be present.
No fever lowers but does NOT exclude SBP, which is frequently afebrile and silent — diagnostic paracentesis is still mandatory.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hepatitis C serology / HCV RNA PCR

investigation_details.investigation_name: Hepatitis C antibody + HCV RNA PCR
investigation_details.expected_result: Anti-HCV positive with detectable HCV RNA (presumed) — confirms chronic infection
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The curable underlying aetiology is missed and antiviral therapy is not offered.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Detectable HCV RNA is diagnostic of active hepatitis C — a laboratory test, never available via telehealth in real time.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diagnostic ascitic tap — high serum-ascites albumin gradient, cell count and culture

investigation_details.expected_result: SAAG >= 11 g/L consistent with portal hypertension; neutrophil count to exclude SBP
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: SBP and the cause of ascites are not characterised — a hospital procedure the AI cannot perform.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms portal-hypertensive ascites and excludes SBP; mandatory for new ascites and only available in hospital.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: decompensated cirrhosis, presumed chronic hepatitis C
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic fatigue + RUQ ache + cachexia + cutaneous stigmata on a blood-borne-virus risk background is the pattern that separates hepatic from cardiac and renal causes.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: tense ascites with mechanical respiratory compromise
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Rapid tense ascites + oedema + orthopnoea is what makes this an urgent, hospital-level decompensation rather than a chronic stable state.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Liver function tests, serum albumin and bilirubin
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify synthetic failure and cholestasis; core to grading severity (Child-Pugh).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and coagulation profile (INR)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Thrombocytopenia supports portal hypertension; INR reflects synthetic function and bleeding risk before any procedure.
Screen for hepatorenal syndrome / AKI and guide safe diuresis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Hepatitis C serology and HCV RNA PCR (plus hepatitis B and HIV)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Establish the curable underlying aetiology and screen co-infections.
Mandatory for new ascites to confirm portal-hypertensive origin and exclude spontaneous bacterial peritonitis; a hospital procedure.
Assess liver morphology, splenomegaly, portal flow and screen for hepatocellular carcinoma.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Upper GI endoscopy for variceal surveillance
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identify and band oesophageal varices to prevent haemorrhage once the patient is stabilised.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical home diuretic trial without assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Diuresis in new tense ascites must be hospital-initiated after bloods and paracentesis; starting it blindly risks hepatorenal syndrome and electrolyte harm.
